summary: numdifftools npm package exfiltrates hostname to webhook.site on install
details: Package's package.json declares a `preinstall` lifecycle hook that runs automatically on `npm install` and issues an HTTPS GET to https://webhook.site/cbfd239f-50d1-4bc6-9801-ba08ba9a2a4a with the installer's hostname appended as a query parameter (`?host=numdiff-<hostname>`). The package's `index.js` is empty (0 bytes) — the package has no legitimate functionality and exists solely to fire the install-time beacon. The package name impersonates the well-known Python `numdifftools` numerical-differentiation library on npm, and the description self-identifies as a 'Bug Bounty PoC'. Regardless of stated intent, every installer that mistakenly fetches this package leaks its host identifier to a third-party canary endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
